title: Masters Week 2026: McIlroy Defends, Scheffler Strikes Back
excerpt: Augusta awakens. Rory McIlroy returns as defending champion against a field led by Scottie Scheffler, Jon Rahm, and Bryson DeChambeau.
body:
The Masters Is Here

The azaleas bloom, the pines frame Amen Corner, and the most watched week in golf begins Thursday. The 2026 Masters brings 90 of the world's elite to Augusta National, and the stakes have rarely felt higher. Defending champion Rory McIlroy arrives fresh from his Champions Dinner menu reveal — a moment of ceremony before the real business begins.

McIlroy: The Man in Green

There is something different about McIlroy this year. After 13 years of near-misses, collapses, and what-ifs, he finally captured the Green Jacket in 2025. That relief, that liberation, has a way of changing a golfer. He arrives not as someone desperate to prove something, but as someone who already has. His game is sharp. His mind is clear. A second consecutive green jacket would cement him as Augusta's contemporary master.

The Contenders

But McIlroy won't have it his own way. Scottie Scheffler, the world No. 1, took a week off ahead of fatherhood but arrives utterly dialled in. Two-time major winner Jon Rahm has quietly rounded into ominous form. Bryson DeChambeau, fresh from back-to-back LIV victories and back-to-back play-off dramatics, carries an unusual confidence. Cameron Young dismantled The Players Championship only two weeks ago. Matt Fitzpatrick won the Valspar immediately after. In other years, these would be the favourites.

Tournament Dates
Apr 9-12
Defending Champion
Rory McIlroy
Field Size
91 Players
Course Par
72

The Narrative

This is McIlroy's tournament now, but it is also everyone else's to take. Scheffler is the favourite — 5/1 in most betting markets — yet Rahm, DeChambeau, and Young lurk nearby. The last four Masters winners have all had previous major championships under their belt. Of the 91 starters, 44 have won majors. This is not a field of pretenders. This is major championship golf at its most concentrated.

Par 3 Contest Today

The Par 3 Contest airs on ESPN Wednesday (2-4 p.m. ET) from the par-3 course. It's the traditional warm-up — famous for its upsets and its occasional harbinger of victory. Nobody has won the Par 3 and the Masters in the same week since Jack Nicklaus in 1965. Augusta's ghosts are watching.

Where to Watch

Streaming on Masters.com, Amazon Prime, Paramount+, and the ESPN App throughout the week. CBS takes over Saturday and Sunday for the final two rounds. For British golf fans, BBC and Sky Sports will have full coverage. First shots Thursday at 1:30 p.m. BST. The Green Jacket waits.
